prompt:
Help frame the research described in the benchmark case as a concrete business-research problem. Preserve the supplied model and identify claims or information that remain unsupported.

Condition instructions:
Respond as you normally would to this user request using only the serialized case material. Do not assume facts that are not supplied.

Benchmark case:
```json
{
  "adversarial": true,
  "decision_time_facts": [
    "risk score",
    "conditional score distributions",
    "payoff weights"
  ],
  "domain": "IS",
  "evidence_needs": [
    {
      "claim": "Conditional score distributions differ by latent type",
      "source_needed": false,
      "status": "model_assumption"
    }
  ],
  "fidelity_requirements": [
    "True account type is not observed at admission",
    "The policy conditions on the score through one common threshold"
  ],
  "hard_failure_traps": [
    "future_or_hidden_information",
    "state_as_decision",
    "model_changed_for_story"
  ],
  "id": "is-11",
  "model_specification": {
    "constraints": [
      "one common threshold for all accounts"
    ],
    "decision_variables": [
      "common risk-score threshold"
    ],
    "mathematical_objective": "maximize expected platform value from benign admissions net of malicious harm and rejection loss",
    "mechanism": "The noisy score changes posterior risk, so the threshold trades false rejection of benign accounts against admission of malicious accounts.",
    "model_type": "Bayesian account-screening threshold",
    "state_variables": [
      "latent benign or malicious account type",
      "observed risk score",
      "admission outcome"
    ],
    "uncertainty": "latent account type and risk-score realization"
  },
  "prohibited_inferences": [
    "The platform directly observes benign or malicious type",
    "The threshold can vary by the account's true type"
  ],
  "prompt": "A platform sets one screening threshold from a noisy account-risk score before admitting users. Benign or malicious type remains latent. A proposed policy applies different thresholds after directly observing each account's true type. Diagnose the hidden-information error and frame the implementable score-based rule.",
  "tags": [
    "platform-screening",
    "latent-type",
    "information-asymmetry",
    "adversarial"
  ],
  "title": "Latent account type treated as an observed screening label"
}
```
